backstory: Mid-level engineer at a 50-person Series B company. Working in a turbo-managed monorepo. Sentry already deployed company-wide. Wants tools that help *them* personally, not the org.

repo_signals.stack: turbo monorepo, next.js + nest.js + postgres + redis

expected_wow: Plan-of-action menu emphasizes personal-scope options: 'Wire @mur/reviewer — your last 3 PRs took avg 4 days to land, structural concerns caught in 2 hours'; 'Set up the daily digest — tomorrow would surface PRs #1421, #1438 you opened (still open)'. The menu is narrower than indie-stripe (catalog and security-audit don't fire — infra-mature stack already covers those slots).
expected_dropoff_risk: Org policy on third-party connects (turn 3). GitHub-App per-repo scoping should help. The 'Detected: gh authed, Sentry SDK, CODEOWNERS' line in scan output reassures that Mur sees their company-mature setup correctly.
wow_score_target: ≥ 2 in H5 (signal specificity), H8 (chief-of-staff voice), H9 (plan breadth — narrow but adapted to mature stack), H10 (plan grounding — every item names a PR # / file)
scoring_notes: Infra-mature persona. H9 score 2 acceptable: only 2-3 plan items will fire (Wire @mur/reviewer, daily digest, maybe Linear-connect prompt). The menu adapts honestly — doesn't pad with security-audit when no risky_patterns exist.
